[TITLE]: Magnetic Sun Archives ~ amy eyrie
[METADATA/INTRO]: The intriguing image above was uploaded to Fine Art America by an astrophotographer, Jason Guenzel, on January 16, 2021. The image is titled— “Magnetic Sun: A stylized rendition of a solar close-up. According to the artist, "the image highlights the...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The intriguing image above was uploaded to Fine Art America by an astrophotographer, Jason Guenzel, on January 16, 2021. The image is titled— “Magnetic Sun: A stylized rendition of a solar close-up. According to the artist, "the image highlights the turbulent magnetic field on the visible surface of our star.” The picture was processed using software to enhance the solar chromosphere or gaseous layer above the sun. Treading the line between art and science, the viral image allows the viewer to conceptualize the sun in new and intriguing ways. Once the overwhelming radiance of the star is drawn down, the resulting image challenges the preconceived notions we have of our closest star, instilling a new sense of wonder at the turbulent force that gives life to planet Earth. This heavily software-processed image of the solar chromosphere reveals the complex nature of the magnetic field within our star.Walking the thin line between science and art ... perhaps blurring it a bit. #Astrophotography #space #solar #star #power pic.twitter.com/DaG3xjEiZd— Jason Guenzel … [Read more...] about Magnetic Sun: Art vs Science

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: Fact Check: Close-up image of the sun is not NASA photo. Here's the real story - Yahoo News Canada
[METADATA/INTRO]: Fact Check: Close-up image of the sun is not NASA photo. Here's the real story Claim:...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Fact Check: Close-up image of the sun is not NASA photo. Here's the real story 
 An authentic NASA photograph shows the surface of the sun. 
 The image was the work of astrophotographer Jason Guenzel, who created it through a mix of real photography and digital editing. 
 An image shared to Facebook in March 2025 claiming to show "the most clearest picture of the sun ... Taken by NASA" garnered more than 266,000 reactions on Facebook at the time of this writing. It showed what appeared to be a close-up image of the sun, with red, orange and white hues across our solar system's central star.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2021-02-20
[TITLE]: Is this the clearest photograph of the Sun taken by NASA? Here’s the truth! - Fact Crescendo Sri Lanka English | The leading fact-checking website
[METADATA/INTRO]: Photos of natural phenomenon are shared and received with great amazement on social media. These unbelievable photos often attract lot of engagements on social media. Sometimes images misattributed to global agencies/celebrities are shared widely as social media users deem them...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: A number of Facebook users shared an image claimed to be of the surface of the sun as seen below along with the caption “The clearest picture of the Sun surface published by NASA” 
 The Sinhala version of this post shared widely claiming the same in the caption “සූර්යයාගේ පැහැදිලිම පින්තූරය NASA ආයතනය මගින් මුදාහැරපු”. Similar posts can be seen in the screenshot of Facebook Search given below: 
 We did a reverse image search of the viral image, which led us to an online art market website called “Fine Art America”. 
 Here we noticed a Michigan based photographer named Jason Guenzel had uploaded the viral image on January 16, 2021, along with the caption “Magnetic Sun: A stylized rendition of a solar close-up. This image highlights the turbulent magnetic field on the visible surface of our star” as seen below in his photo album titled “Solar System”
